column1: # THE STAGE CONTRACT — single source of truth for the factory line (SPEC-016 / TICKET-067).
# Follows the ruleset-map.tsv precedent (ADR-0006): a reviewable data file the scripts read,
# not a code generator. Before this file the Stage vocabulary existed in FIVE copies, in three
# different shapes — setup-project.sh, board-sync.sh, metrics.sh, setup-repo.sh and
# PROJECTS.md — held in step by a comment saying "must match setup-project.sh". They had
# already drifted: PROJECTS.md documented a different seven-option vocabulary entirely.
# COLUMNS (tab-separated):
#   1  stage-label   the `stage:*` GitHub label, or `-` for an option with no label
#   2  Stage-option  the Projects v2 "Stage" single-select option
#   3  color         label colour (hex, no `#`); empty when column 1 is `-`
#   4  description   label description; empty when column 1 is `-`
# READ BY:
#   setup-project.sh  --single-select-options  <- column 2, deduped, IN THIS ORDER
#   board-sync.sh     label -> Stage lookup    <- columns 1,2
#   metrics.sh        STAGES array             <- column 2, deduped, in order
#   setup-repo.sh     stage:* label rows       <- columns 1,3,4
#   PROJECTS.md       documented vocabulary    <- asserted against this file by the test
# RULES
#   - ORDER IS SIGNIFICANT. Column 2 deduped top-to-bottom IS the board's option order, and
#     the options are byte-identical to the pre-SPEC-016 script. Do not reorder or rename to
#     "tidy" them: renaming a Projects v2 option does not migrate existing cards, and the
#     upgrade path is additive-only.
#   - Two labels MAY map to one option (spec/spec-review -> Spec; verify/audit -> Verify).
#   - An option with `-` in column 1 is INTENTIONALLY UNMAPPED: it is set by means other than
#     label sync (see below), not broken. board-sync.sh exits 0 on a label it cannot map.
#   - Adding an option here without thinking through who WRITES it produces an empty column.
#     The board is additive-only; an unused field/option cannot later be removed.
# INTENTIONALLY UNMAPPED OPTIONS (spec AC5) — set by other means, not by `stage:*` label sync:
#   Brief     pre-spec intake; an item sits here before it has a stage: label at all
#   Tickets   the tech-lead breakdown step; no label marks it (specs carry stage:spec throughout)
#   Deploy    driven by deploy.yml environment progression, not by an issue label
#   Observe   post-deploy monitoring; entered by incident/observation, not by a label
# stage-label
stage:spec
stage:spec-review
column4: spec gate — waiting on human approval before build

column1: stage:code
stage:test
stage:verify
stage:audit
stage:review
stage:done
